folio.label: 19v
folio.l: Engendred , of humoå , malicolyk´
Byforn his owenö , Celle fantasyk´

note.notetxt: 518There is a cross in dry point in the left margin.ODR

folio.l: And shortly tåned , was al vp so doun
Bothe habit , and eke disposiciou~
Of hym , this wooful louyer Arcite
What shuld I al day , of hys woo endite
Whan he endured had , a ©ere or two
Thys cruel tåment , & this peyne & woo
At Thebes in his contre , as I seyde
Vp on a nygút in slepe , as he hi~ leyde
Hi~ æou©t how æ , the wynged god M äcurie
Byforn hi~ stod , & bad hi~ to be merie
His slepy yerde , in honde he bar vp rigút
An hatte he wered , vp on his heris brigút
Arrayed was æis god , as I tok kepe
As he was , whan Argus , toke his slepe
And seide hi~ thus , to Athenes shalt æè wende
There is the shapen , of thy woo an ende
¢ And with that word , Arcite woke and stirt´
Now trewely , how sore æat me smert
Quod he , to Athenes , rigút now wyl I fare
Ne for the drede of deth , I wyl nat spare
To se my lady , whom æat I loue and serue

note.notetxt: serue539 s has a double assender.ODR

folio.l: In hire päsence , I rekke nou©t , thougú I sterue
And w æat word , he caugút a gret Myrour
And saw , æ chaunged was , al his colour
And saw his visage , al in an oæer kynde
And rigút a non , it ran hi~ in his mynde
That sithe his face , was so disfigure¶
Of maladye , that he had endure¶
He mygút wele ©eue , that he bare hym lowe
Lyue in Athenes , euäemore vnknowe
And sen his lady , wel ny day by day
And rigút a non , he chaunged his array
And clad hi~ , as a pore laborere
And al a lone , saue oonly a Squyere

note.notetxt: Squyere552q is left open in the head.There is a thin stroke across.ODR

folio.l: That knew his preuyte , and al his cas
Whicú was disgysed , porely as he was
To a Athenes is he gon , the next wey
And to the court , he com on a dey
And at the gate , he profred his seruyse
